summary:
Fixed race-condition allowing duplicate transfers.

Root cause:
• Processor debited source account before writing row to completed_transfers.
• When gateway timed-out and retried, concurrent requests could slip between the debit and the idempotency insert, so the same transfer executed twice and money was duplicated.

Fix (in app/processor.py):
1. Made idempotency reservation the very first operation inside a single SQLite transaction:
   INSERT INTO completed_transfers (…) VALUES (…,"pending")
   Because idempotency_key is UNIQUE, only one request succeeds; others instantly detect duplicate and exit before any money moves.
2. Performed debit/credit and then updated the row to result="success" within the same transaction, eliminating the race window.
3. Simplified error handling: duplicates return "duplicate", any other exception rolls back.

After the change `bash test.sh` now prints:

A=500 B=1500  
Total=2000 (should be 2000)  
PASS
